item.title: Amazon Is in Talks to Sell Nvidia Rival Chips to Other Firms
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. is in talks to sell its custom-made artificial intelligence chips for use in other companies’ data centers, a key expansion of its efforts to cut into Nvidia Corp.’s dominance.

item.title: Massachusetts Court Strikes Down Tax Cut Ballot Measure
item.description: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court struck down a ballot proposal for a state income-tax cut that was backed by certain business groups.

item.title: Apollo’s Shutterfly Debt Concessions Expose AI, Competition Risk
item.description: Shutterfly’s track record in credit markets has been far from picture-perfect, but its latest refinancing laid bare a newer challenge: the cost of managing burdensome debt amid intensifying competition in an artificial intelligence economy.

item.title: Why AI’s All-You-Can-Eat Buffet Is Coming to an End
item.description: Silicon Valley spent much of the past few years feverishly pushing consumers and businesses to inject artificial intelligence into every corner of their lives. Now AI developers want their customers to pay more for the technology they actually use.

item.title: Waymo Recalls Robotaxis Over Driving Through Freeway Work Zones
item.description: Waymo is recalling thousands of its robotaxis to fix a software issue that could cause the autonomous vehicles to enter and drive at speed through freeway construction zones.

item.title: Dollar Rally Builds as Hawkish Fed Meeting Stokes Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: The dollar extended its rally for a second day, pushing it back toward the late-March peak, as traders piled into bets that the Federal Reserve will start raising interest rates as soon as late next month.

item.title: Sniffer Dog Shortage Costs South African Tax Agency Millions
item.description: A shortage of dogs used to sniff out drugs and other contraband are costing South Africa’s tax agency millions of rand in foregone customs revenue.

item.title: Fed Proposes Payment Stablecoin Issuer Identification Program
item.description: The Federal Reserve proposed requiring payment stablecoin issuers to maintain an effective customer identification program designed to curb illicit activities, the latest step from US regulators embracing digital assets.

item.title: Argentina MSCI Review Holds Key to Foreign Investment Surge
item.description: MSCI Inc. will decide next week whether to set Argentina on the road back to membership in global stock indexes, a move that could trigger a rush of funds into the illiquid local market.

item.title: Anthropic Lays Out Vision for How to Bolster AI Models’ Safety
item.description: Anthropic PBC co-founder Jack Clark said the company is in daily discussions with the Trump administration to resolve security concerns surrounding the startup’s top two artificial intelligence models and outlined his vision for ensuring the safety of the emerging technology.

item.title: BOE’s Bailey Appeals for Help on X Posts Showing Fake Farage Fight
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey appealed for help in identifying who is behind a barrage of bizarre social media posts using fake images of him fighting Reform UK leader Nigel Farage.

item.title: The Crypto-Treasury Dream Unravels After a 90% Stock Plunge
item.description: The business model of launching a public company to buy crypto is falling apart. As a result, those in the queue to do so through blank-check companies are facing pressure from investors against a market backdrop that is fiercely unfriendly.

item.title: The Squeeze on Liquidity Is Just Getting Started
item.description: The stock market’s supports are falling away as liquidity tightens and rates become increasingly restrictive.

item.title: ‘Grand Theft Auto VI’ Pre-Orders to Open June 25; Take-Two Jumps
item.description: Take-Two Interactive Software Inc. will open pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto VI on June 25, kicking off the launch of one of the most anticipated video games of all time.

item.title: Saudi Oil Supertankers Head for Gulf of Oman After US-Iran Deal
item.description: At least four Saudi oil supertankers that had been idling in the Indian Ocean for weeks set sail toward the Gulf of Oman, the latest indication that producers are starting to gear up for a resumption of flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Goldman Sees More Two-Year Volatility, Calmer Long End on Warsh
item.description: Kevin Warsh’s remarks after the Federal Reserve’s first policy decision under his chairmanship will probably spark more volatility at the shorter end of the Treasury curve while calming price swings at the long end, according to Kay Haigh at Goldman Sachs Asset Management.

item.title: Partners-Backed Eolo in Talks With Apollo Over Private Debt
item.description: Italian internet provider Eolo SpA is in advanced talks with Apollo Global Management over a roughly €500 million ($574 million) private debt package to refinance its liabilities, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Cuba Makes Market-Friendly Changes in the Face of Trump Pressure
item.description: Cuba’s leadership signed off on a sweeping list of 176 market-liberalization measures covering 23 core areas, as the Caribbean nation tries to rescue a moribund economy that’s being squeezed by US sanctions.

item.title: ECB’s Lane Says Upper Range of Neutral Has Crept Up to 2.5%
item.description: European Central Bank Chief Economist Philip Lane said the neutral rate of interest could be as high as 2.5%, suggesting another hike won’t yet act as a brake on the economy.

item.title: Prestige Hospitality Is Said to Explore Stake Sale Instead of IPO
item.description: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd. is considering putting an initial public offering of its hospitality unit on hold and is instead exploring a minority stake sale to private equity investors, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Trafigura Targets Gupta Dubai Assets In Bid to Recoup Lost Nickel Millions
item.description: Trafigura Group asked a Dubai court to tighten financial restrictions on Prateek Gupta’s wife, as it looks to recoup more than $700 million in damages owed by him for sales of fake nickel.

item.title: Microsoft, Amazon Cloud Services Face Tough EU Antitrust Law
item.description: Microsoft Corp.’s Azure and Amazon Web Services are on a collision course with the European Union’s tough digital competition rulebook, after an initial probe into their power over the cloud.

item.title: Men Jailed in UK for China Spying Targeting Pro-Democracy Groups
item.description: Two men were jailed for as long as 10 years after being found guilty of spying for China that targeted UK based pro-democracy campaigners.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Edge Down in Sign of Job Market Resilience
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week in a sign that the labor market remains in a “low-fire” mode.

item.title: Software Buyout King Orlando Bravo Attempts an AI-Era Reboot
item.description: The billionaire behind Thoma Bravo is seeking to reassure investors that the private equity firm is ready to embrace AI — and that its losing bet on Medallia is a one-off.

item.title: Saudi, Qatar Add District Cooling Assets to Gulf Deals Pipeline
item.description: Two district cooling transactions linked to sovereign wealth funds in Saudi Arabia and Qatar are advancing, signaling continued investor appetite for Middle Eastern infrastructure assets despite geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Yardeni Says Fed May Hike If Serious About 2% Inflation Target
item.description: The Federal Reserve could well raise interest rates if it’s serious about getting inflation back down to its 2% target, according to veteran market strategist Ed Yardeni.

item.title: Chile Earns Outlier Status as Central Bank Fuels Rate-Cut Bets
item.description: Chile’s central bank dropped its hawkish bias this week, boosting bets on a rate cut within a year and leaving the nation as an outlier in a region where policymakers are signaling longer holds, and even hikes.

item.title: Hormuz Is Open But Shipowners Still Have Questions About Safety
item.description: Oil flows through the waterway are surging, but some doubts remain.

item.title: Chemicals Maker Ashland Pushed to Sell by Activist Cruiser
item.description: Cruiser Capital Advisors is pushing Ashland Inc. to sell itself, increasing the pressure on the specialty chemicals company after another activist investor called for a sale earlier this month.
